channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Mutfwang’s reinstatement of Plateau monarch will consolidate peace, say community leaders
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Community leaders say Governor Mutfwang’s reinstatement of the Rekna of Bashar will consolidate peace and unity in Plateau State. Read more here.
Read More: https://punchng.com/mutfwangs-reinstatement-of-plateau-monarch-will-consolidate-peace-say-community-leaders/

item.title: Mary Habila has never been ‘a runs girl,’ family defends dead nurse
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Mary Habila’s family defends her character, appealing to the IGP for the immediate release of the nurse’s body for burial after her controversial death.

item.title: Herbert leads The Open after historic round and DeChambeau rules drama
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lucas Herbert holds a two-shot lead after an extraordinary second round at The Open, where two players equalled major history and Bryson DeChambeau was involved in a controversial ruling.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The security team engaged the abductors in a fierce gun battle, during which one of the suspected kidnappers was fatally shot, forcing the remaining gang members to flee into the forest and abandon their captive.

item.title: Transfer: Super Eagles winger swaps club in Russia

item.description: Tammy Beaumont and Sarah Bryce put on a 118-run stand to lead the Blaze to a dominant 10-wicket win over Durham to secure the Women's T20 Blast title at the Kia Oval.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: I racked up $35,000 in credit-card debt. Should I file for bankruptcy?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I’m also considering working with a credit-counseling agency or enrolling in a hardship program.”

item.title: Martin Shkreli Is Back, Shorting Stocks and Taking On Everyone
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Martin Shkreli wants to make one thing clear to anyone who’s willing to listen: He’s back and he still doesn’t care how many people he ticks off along the way.

item.title: Make affordable housing a national economic priority, AIHS urges FG
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Africa International Housing Show (AIHS), organisers of Africa’s largest housing and construction exhibition, has called on President Bola Ahmed read more Make affordable housing a national economic priority, AIHS urges FG

item.title: 2027: Malami Picks Musa Zagi As ADC Deputy Governorship Candidate In Kebbi
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The party congratulated Zagi on his emergence and expressed confidence in his capacity to effectively discharge the responsibilities of the office if elected. The post 2027: Malami Picks Musa Zagi As ADC Deputy Governorship Candidate In Kebbi appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: 2027 polls: NNPP raises concerns about potential misuse of state police
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Johnbosco Agbakwuru, Abuja A fresh controversy has erupted over the recently passed State Police Bill after Prince Nweze Onu, Deputy National Chairman of the New Nigeria People’s Party (NNPP), warned that the proposed security reform could be turned into a dangerous political weapon by state gove

item.title: Police secure conviction of 16 for vandalising govt property in Delta
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Delta State Police Command secures conviction of 16 suspects for vandalising government property, theft, and drug possession. Read more on the Delta vandal
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-secure-conviction-of-16-for-vandalising-govt-property-in-delta/

item.title: Hedge Funds Add Bullish Oil Bets at Fastest Pace in a Decade
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Hedge funds piled into bullish bets on Brent oil at the fastest pace in almost a decade as fighting between Iran and the US ramped up, dampening investors’ expectations of a sustained recovery in shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: CDC and FDA link lettuce at Taco Bell in five states to cyclospora outbreak
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: FDA investigation identified a single supplier of the lettuce, but federal warnings did not name the companyFederal health officials have identified lettuce from Mexico served by Taco Bell locations across five US states as a source of the widespread outbreak of the diarrhea-causing parasite cyclosp

item.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.

item.title: NECO Defends Kogi School, Rejects ‘Miracle Centre’ Label After Abduction Incident
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Examinations Council (NECO) has dismissed claims that Government Secondary School, Olowa, in Dekina Local Government Area of Kogi State, is a “miracle centre,” insisting that the institution is a legitimate public school that has consistently presented candidates for the Sen

item.title: Mary Habila’s father petitions IGP, seeks release of daughter’s body
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Inspector General of Police (IGP) Tunji Disu has been petitioned to take over the investigation into the death of Mary Habila, a staff member of David Umahi Federal University of Health Sciences, Uburu, Ebonyi State, following allegations that the Ebonyi State Police Command has unlawfully refus

item.title: UPDATED: FG arraigns three suspects over kidnapping of Oyo pupils, teachers
